input.issue_title: Security issue - double free in parse_object
input.issue_body: Hi during a fuzzing session using PyJFuzz, i found that is possible to trigger a double free condition when parse_object function is called, the double free happend inside parse_string, below a screenshot Below the testcase i used to crash cJSON > [{"FsrKY7": {"xsatsIjrY": {"f4UOmTp": -59.634942997}, "qf6t2w7f0": {}, "vwY7wMW": {"mcyrBl": "X1tjR5d", "m\FsFF20": "UzbJl"}}, "FGRF1wI": {"R6KZm": 127, "jC8utX": null}, "6nrvXK9sk": {"Gj7zP": {}}}, {"V5GD8GR": -19.5946741823, "bYsh2vyhp": -24.3975015443}{"V5GD8GR": -19.5946741823, "bYsh2vyhp": 24.3975015443}, ] and the output I don't investigate further, so please let me know what do you think. Best regards, Daniele

cJSON.C   Line-1115
If  hooks->reallocate  failed   and  set 'buffer->buffer'  to  NULL without free.

input.pr_title: Allocate memory for the temporary buffer when paring numbers
input.pr_body:
Currently a temporary buffer with size of 64 is used when parsing numbers. Long numbers with length of 64 or more could not be correctly parsed, as CVE-2023-26819 described.

This PR allocate memory for the temporary buffer when paring numbers.
This also fixes CVE-2023-26819.
